Root cause was two independent viewport-height calculations: BrowserOrchestrator.GetReaderViewportHeight used terminalHeight-3 while TerminalPageRenderer.RenderReadable used terminalHeight-_helpers.LinesWritten-2 (LinesWritten=3 after header → terminalHeight-5). 2-line mismatch made the cursor underline invisible past the rendered bottom. Fix: new ReaderLayout helper (HeaderLines=3, StatusBarReservedLines=2, ComputeContentHeight) wired into both consumers. Extracted pure ScrollForCursor for tested invariant math. Anti-recurrence: ReaderLayoutInvariantTests.HeaderLines_MatchesActualLineCountWrittenByLinkTreeRenderer renders the real header and asserts the constant matches actual lines — any future header change fails the test before the speed-read symptom returns. Regression: SpeedReadCursorVisibilityTests.PreFix_Mismatch_DemonstratesCursorInvisibility reconstructs the bug and asserts invisible-cursor count > 0 then re-runs post-fix and asserts invariant holds. Tests: 19 umi7-relevant + 3243 full suite + live termtest at 100x35 and 100x24 confirmed cursor visible across 25/30 ticks. QA-enforcer approved on 1st review (live termtest performed by reviewer).
